description: Universal usability requirements based on Nielsen's heuristics. Ensures every interface is learnable, recoverable, and transparent.

requirements.text: Every interactive element (button, icon, link) has a discoverable label — visible text, tooltip, or aria-label
requirements.intent: Users must understand what every interactive element does
requirements.verifiable_by: Audit interactive elements for accessible names; no unlabeled controls

requirements.text: Every user-initiated action produces immediate feedback (loading/disabled state) and completion feedback (result message, navigation change, or visible state update)
requirements.intent: Users must receive feedback after actions and understand what happened
requirements.verifiable_by: Trigger each action; confirm loading indicator appears within 200ms and completion feedback follows

requirements.text: Modals, dialogs, forms, and multi-step workflows provide cancel, close, or back paths
requirements.intent: Users must be able to exit or cancel operations
requirements.verifiable_by: Open each modal/dialog/form; confirm escape route exists and works

requirements.text: Destructive actions (delete, reset, remove, overwrite) require explicit confirmation or provide undo within a reasonable window
requirements.intent: Users must be protected from irreversible mistakes
requirements.verifiable_by: Trigger each destructive action; confirm confirmation dialog or undo mechanism exists

requirements.text: Error messages are human-readable, explain what went wrong, and suggest a next step or recovery action
requirements.intent: Users must understand errors and how to recover
requirements.verifiable_by: Trigger known error conditions; confirm messages are actionable, not raw stack traces or codes

requirements.text: Required inputs are marked, input constraints (format, length, allowed values) are visible before submission, and available options are discoverable
requirements.intent: Users must not need to remember hidden information
requirements.verifiable_by: Review all forms; confirm required markers, format hints, and validation rules are visible pre-submit

requirements.text: Pages and flows provide contextual orientation — page titles, breadcrumbs, highlighted navigation state, or step indicators
requirements.intent: Users must always know where they are in the interface
requirements.verifiable_by: Navigate each route; confirm title updates and navigation state reflects current location

requirements.text: Interactive elements meet WCAG 2.2 minimum target size (24x24 CSS px) and are visually distinguishable from non-interactive content
requirements.intent: Users must be able to interact comfortably
requirements.verifiable_by: Measure tap/click targets; confirm minimum 24x24px and visual affordance (border, color, cursor)

requirements.text: Core user flows are fully keyboard-navigable with visible focus indicators on all interactive elements
requirements.intent: Users must be able to operate the interface without a mouse
requirements.verifiable_by: Tab through each core flow; confirm focus is visible and all actions reachable via keyboard

requirements.text: Text and interactive element colors meet WCAG AA contrast ratio (minimum 4.5:1 for normal text, 3:1 for large text and UI components)
requirements.intent: Content must be readable for users with low vision or in bright environments
requirements.verifiable_by: Run automated contrast check (axe-core, Lighthouse); confirm no AA violations on text or interactive elements

requirements.text: Touch targets on mobile meet platform guidelines (minimum 44x44pt on iOS, 48x48dp on Android)
requirements.intent: Mobile users must be able to tap targets accurately without frustration
requirements.verifiable_by: Audit touch targets on mobile viewports; confirm minimum 44x44pt sizing
